rows.notes: Year is clearly written centered at top of page; no monetary columns present on this line.
Title underlined and clear; no amounts on the title line itself (serves as section header).
Large number at very top right (3581 4 1). Appears to be an opening balance/total; columns (£ s d) are well aligned here and digits are legible.
Description reads 'Bursar P. Tax'. Amount reads 107 pounds, 9 shillings, 2 pence. Last pence digit slightly crowded by ledger rule but alignment with column lines supports this reading.
Description clear. Amount aligned as 58 10 0; numerals are distinct and within valid ranges.
Entry text is crossed through but still legible as 'Open Exhibition'. Amount reads 90 0 0 though the line is struck; I record it but reduced confidence due to strike-through.
Name reads 'Carling Snow' (handwriting clear). Amount columns show 8 pounds, 1 shilling, 3 pence; digits are distinct and aligned.
Single-name entry 'Osborne'. Amount 16 7 6 is clear and within valid ranges.
Reads 'Self as Rector'. Amount 73 2 6 clearly aligned in £ s d columns.
Description reads 'Whitehead 1/2 Int on £200'. Amount is 3 pounds 18 shillings 0 pence; 18 shillings is within valid range and digits are legible.

Handwritten description looks like 'Roots for Garden' (first letter could be R or B; chosen 'Roots' based on letter shape). Amount aligned as 1 8 0; moderate confidence due to slight ambiguity in the initial letter of description.
Description 'Advertisement' is legible. Pounds column blank; shillings and pence read as 6 and 8 respectively (6s 8d). Alignment shows no pounds value.
Entry reads 'Subrector Easter Sermon'. Amount 2 2 0 is clearly written and aligned.
Description legible as 'Domus private Bill'. Amount 2 pounds, 15 shillings, 11 pence is clear in the columns.
Text reads 'Duke Gravel and turning Dr' (last word 'Dr' interpreted from abbreviation); amount 14 10 0 is clear.
Entry 'Osborn New Wall' with amount 13 0 0 clearly aligned and legible.

Handwritten description appears to read 'Kedlington Chapel repair' (spelling uncertain). Amount occupies shillings/pence columns as 2s 6d; pounds column blank. Description reading uncertain so confidence reduced.
Reads 'Printing Univty prep' (abbreviated 'Univty' inferred as 'University'). Amount 1 9 3 is legible although the script is somewhat cursive.
Description 'Rowles draining Moor' is reasonably clear. Amount 20 0 0 is distinct and aligned.
Handwritten label looks like 'Baxter superintendency Edw Garden' (several words are abbreviated/uncertain, esp. the latter part). Amount 3 3 0 is clear.
Single-word label 'Bursar' clearly written; no amount on this line. Serves as a parent label for subsequent sub-items.
Sub-line under 'Bursar' reads approximately 'Domus Battels' (spelling uncertain—could be 'Battels' or 'Battels'). Amount 83 9 11 is aligned and legible; pence 11 is within valid range.
Description looks like 'Impers Colle' or 'Imprs Colle' (abbreviation/word uncertain). Amount 62 13 6 is readable but handwriting on label is ambiguous.

Text 'Poor Rate returned' readable. Amount reads '2 18 6' in the image but the pence digit is ambiguous due to smudge/ink — I have interpreted this as 2 pounds 18 shillings 6 pence previously; however, shillings of 18 would normally be in the shilling column and the visual spacing is tight. (Because 18 appears in shilling position and 6 in pence position on the page, but alignment here is crowded, I mark confidence low.)

The label reads 'Percentage' (clear) but the numeric alignment for this line is problematic: the digits to the right read '29 7 0' visually, however 29 shillings would be invalid (>=20). This indicates the left-most of the three numeric columns likely contains '29' as pounds, not shillings. I therefore interpret this as 29 pounds, 7 shillings, 0 pence. Because of initial misalignment risk, confidence is low.
Reads 'Ext. & Charges' (abbreviation expanded). Amount aligned as 21 5 0; digits are clear.

A numeric line '2 8 0' (interpreted as 2s 8d or 2 pounds 8 shillings 0 pence depending on alignment) appears with no clear description. The pounds column is narrow here; based on spacing it looks like 2 pounds 8 shillings 0 pence might be intended, but alignment is ambiguous. I record pounds as blank and shillings 2, pence 8 to reflect uncertainty; confidence is low due to unclear alignment and missing label.
Line reads 'Wren Posts in Savin Field' (dated at left). No monetary amount on this line; the text is relatively clear.
Text reads approximately 'Guynear Allowance at audit 4.15.9' but wording is somewhat cramped/abbreviated. No direct amount on this line; lower confidence due to handwriting abbreviations.
Line 'Receipt and Postage' is legible. Amount shown as 12 shillings 6 pence (no pounds); columns align to shillings/pence making this reading clear.
Abbreviated label 'Comm:' (commission). Amount reads 13 16 7; digits are legible but the label is abbreviated which reduces descriptive certainty slightly.

A summed figure '19 4 10' appears to the right of the small 'Receipt/Postage/Comm' group as a combined amount. No description preceding it; alignment and value are clear though contextual assignment as a subtotal is inferred.

Reads 'Tarnell Probationer' (name could be 'Farnell' or 'Tarnell' but initial letter resembles T). Amount 40 0 0 is clear; moderate confidence for the name reading.

Description is partially abbreviated and ambiguous: reads roughly 'Field & cattle collecting Tithe' (could be different exact phrasing). Amount 11 5 0 is clear in numeric columns; the descriptive certainty is moderate.
Line 'Stamps' is clear and amount shows 8 shillings 4 pence (pounds column blank). Numbers are distinct and well aligned.
Final bottom-right page total reads 4509 13 1; digits are large and well aligned, suggesting a page-total. Very clear.
